entry.title: SAP BTP Hackathon 2024 ファイナリストチーム座談会 Vol.2
entry.content:
はじめに

本ブログは「SAP BTP Hackathon 2024」において、１次選考を通過し、ファイナリストとなった三井情報株式会社様（以下、三井情報）、FPTソフトウェアジャパン株式会社様（以下、FPT）、株式会社JSOL様（以下、JSOL）の3社によって行われた座談会についての記事です。

「SAP BTP Hackathon 2024」は、SAPジャパン株式会社の主催するSAP Business Technology Platform (以下、BTP) の利用促進・技術向上を目的としたパートナー/お客様向けのハッカソンイベントです。3年目の開催となった今年のハッカソンは、41社53チーム341名が参加されました。

2024年4月よりEnablementセッションを実施し、同年6月より1週間のハッカソンを行いました。その後SAPジャパンによる選考を経て、SAPジャパン本社会場にて一次選考を突破したファイナリストチーム6組がたくさんの参加者が見守る中で、渾身のプレゼンテーションを行い、6/27(木)に最優秀作品とチームを決める最終選考イベントを実施いたしました。

「SAP BTP Hackathon 2024」の終了から1か月ほど経った今、ファイナリストの６社様を2組に分け、座談会という形で現在の率直な思いを赤裸々にお話しいただきました。

 

チーム/インタビュー参加者紹介

1. FPTソフトウェアジャパン株式会社様（F-ONET）

チーム全体写真（写真左）のレ・ティ・フエン・チャン氏、グエン・ロン・スイエン氏、グエン・カイン・チャウ氏、ファム・タイン・ティエン氏、レ・ドゥク・ティエン氏、ドアン・マン・クイン氏、ファン・ドゥック・ティン氏、グエン・ティ・トゥイ氏、レ・ゴック・タイン・トゥイ氏の９名で構成されたチーム。

インタビューには以下の3名にご参加いただきました。（写真右）

グエン・ロン・スエン氏： エンタープライズビジネスサービス部所属。BTPでの開発と環境構築に従事。今回のハッカソンではチームリーダーとしての参加。

グエン・カイン・チャウ氏：同部署にて、BTPでの開発と環境構築に従事。

グエン・ティ・トゥ氏：同部署にて、BTPでの開発に従事。SAPコンサル歴は1年半ほど。

 

2. 三井情報株式会社様（MKI BTP Mavens）

チーム全体写真（写真左）の矢作氏、宮崎氏、外越氏、家原氏、伊藤氏の５名で構成されたチーム。

インタビューには以下の3名にご参加いただきました。（写真右）

矢作氏：基幹ソリューション技術部所属。SAP S/4HANAのプリセールスや導入PMに従事。SAPコンサル歴は20年、BTPは半年ほど。

家原氏： 同部署にて、SAP S/4HANAの会計領域の導入に従事。SAPコンサル歴は10か月、BTPに関しては今回が初。

外越氏： 同部署所属にて、BTPのプリセールスや技術検証に従事。SAPコンサル歴は3年、BTP経歴は1年ほど。

 

3. 株式会社JSOL様（Approval Wizards）

チーム全体写真（写真左）の奥井氏、中尾氏、中村氏、原川氏、小暮氏、宗森氏、内海氏、柴田氏、古屋氏の９名で構成されたチーム。

インタビューには以下の3名にご参加いただきました。（写真右）

中村氏：プラットフォーム事業本部。Basis領域の開発に従事。SAPコンサル歴は1年ほど。BTPでの開発は今回が初。

奥井氏：クラウドテクノロジー＆イノベーション部所属。システム間の連携に関するソリューション開発に従事。BTP歴は1年ほど。

中尾氏：ストラテジックパートナーアライアンス推進部所属。SAP S/4HANAの会計領域やBW/4 HANAの導入に従事。SAP歴は5年。BTP歴は昨年のハッカソンからはじめ、1年ほど。

 

 

インタビュー内容

本インタビューでは、今回のハッカソンについて、各社開発したソリューションについてのディスカッション、BTPについての大きく３つに分けてファイナリストの皆様にお話を伺いました。

 

１次選考を通過されたときの感想と社内での反応を教えてください。

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：１次選考通過の連絡をいただいたときは驚きました。というのも、昨年もFPTは一次選考を通過していたのですが、今年は昨年と比較して開発にかけられた時間が短く、自信が持てませんでした。結果をチームに共有した時は皆で喜びました。社内では上長に報告したところ、「よかった」「優勝目指して頑張ってください」と励ましの言葉をいただきました。

矢作氏(三井情報）：今回私たちは初めての参加でしたので、それほど大きなプレッシャーには感じていませんでしたが、チーム全員が力をいれて取り組んでくれていたので結果が出てよかったなと素直に思いました。１次選考通過の連絡をいただいたときはちょうど会議が始まる直前で、上司とともに確認して皆で喜びました。社内にも共有をしたところ、皆喜んでくれ、また決勝も応援してくれていました。

奥井氏(JSOL)：1次選考通過の連絡をいただいた時は、半分は嬉しい、半分はほっとしたという感情でした。社内では広域に1次選考通過の発表があったので、周りから「見たよ！」「一次選考通過したんだね！」と声をかけていただくことも多かったです。また、BTPについても興味を持ってもらうことが多く、「BTPってどんなサービスなの？」「来年、ハッカソン出てみようかな」というような声も聞こえてきたのでとても嬉しかったです。

 

開発した作品についてのディスカッション

今回のハッカソンの開発テーマは「SAPソリューションのラストワンマイルを解決する」というテーマでした。そのテーマを振り返りながら、各社様が開発されたソリューションについてそれぞれもう一度説明いただき、お互いに感想や質問などのフィードバックを行い、ソリューションについてディスカッションを行いました 。

1. FPTソフトウェアジャパン株式会社様（F-ONET） ソリューション名：３F- FPT Fast flow

AIを活用し、入庫業務、倉庫管理、品目管理の効率化及びコスト削減を実現するソリューション。

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：私たちはAIを活用した入庫業務をサポートするソリューションを開発しました。まず。事前に入庫する商品の映像や画像を登録し、数量、名称、品質をAIモデルで確認します。その後、問題がなければ自動的に入庫登録がされるようになっています。

矢作氏（三井情報）： 今回のアーキテクチャでGoogleのFirebase や AWSと連携をされていましたが、これは普段の業務からこのような開発を行われたりすでにお客様に提供されたりしているのでしょうか？

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：お客様にSAP Build(※1)を提案する案件はまだないので、今回のハッカソンを通してチームメンバーで学習しました。

西山（SAP）：経験がない中で学習するのはなかなか難しいと思うのですが、どのように学習されていったのですか？

グエン・カイン・チャウ氏：独自にWebで調べたり、SAPから提供いただいたコンテンツなどを通して学習を進めました。また、ベトナムの本社にAI専門のチームがあったので、本社を巻き込んでAIの講習を組んでもらって学習したりしました。

グエン・ティ・トゥ氏：実際に開発を進めていくなかで、学習も含めて大変だった部分も多かったです。特にAIで品質を管理するには事前に破損状態のものも学習させる必要があるのですが、今回デモで採用したのが缶ビールだったこともあり、「缶ビールの破損状態って何？」とチームで頭を悩ませたのが記憶に残っています(笑)。

2. 三井情報株式会社様（MKI BTP Mavens） ソリューション名：TORISUKE

取引先マスタ登録時の困りごとを補助し、上長・管理部門担当者含めたワークフローで完結するソリューション。

 

 

矢作氏（三井情報）：私たちは社内の手続きとして取引先登録を行うときの登録準備（すでに登録されていないか、反社チェック）と承認ワークフローを回すことのできるソリューションを開発しました。課題設定のきっかけは実際に自社の得意先登録が非効率な状態になっていることからで、このソリューションを活用する事でユーザー側はこれ１つで業務が完結するように出来ています。AIは、反社チェックと登録情報の自動提案に活用しています。

中尾氏（JSOL）：当日の発表でも登録時の二重チェックなどが削減できると聞いていて、こういうものがあったら便利だなと思っていました。この先さらにBTPを活用してこんな機能の拡張を行いたいなどといった話はチームの中で出ているのでしょうか？

外越氏（三井情報）：ハッカソンの途中からアイデアは出ていたのですが、実装まで出来なかったものがいくつかありました。1つは得意先の住所の読み込みの際に、現在はWebから情報を取得しているのですが、実際の業務のことを考えると相手方から頂いた名刺から入力するケースも多いと思うので、そこの部分をAI-OCRなどを活用して入力できるようにしたいです。もう1つはすでに登録されている得意先の確認で曖昧検索を実装できていないので実装してみたいです。

家原氏（三井情報）： これから実装したいことではないのですが、実際にハッカソンの開発をしている際に、社内の方から「ちょうどこの間この社内手続きの業務やったよ、このソリューション欲しかったな」といった声も聞かれたので、まずは社内にリリースをして、その後、社外に売り出していけたらいいなとも思いました。

 

 3. 株式会社JSOL様（Approval Wizards） ソリューション名：RobotFill

経費承認の判断に必要な情報を承認者へ提供することで、経費承認の業務がワンタッチで完了するソリューション。

 

奥井氏(JSOL)：我々は経理承認に特化したソリューションを開発しました。機能としては事前同意の確認機能や金額の相場などを調べる外部情報検索機能、社内ドキュメントを検索できる機能などを実装しています。これによって経費申請業務に人の手が介在している部分を無くすことができると考えました。

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：アーキテクチャのことで質問なのですが、今回開発で使われたSAP Business Application Studio(※2)はソースコードを触る必要があると思うのですが、実装に難しさは感じませんでしたか？

奥井氏(JSOL)：そうですね、正直なところ難しさはありました(笑)。 ただ、一番難しいだろうと思っていた外部サービスとの連携の部分がある程度コネクタで準備されていたので、思っていたより楽だったという感覚があります。

外越氏（三井情報）： 最近はメールだけでなくてチャットでのやり取りが多く、またチャットの中でもチームや個人というところがあるのでそこから情報を取得できるのはすごく魅力的だと思いました。また目の付け所として我々は申請者にフォーカスをしましたが、承認者にフォーカスを当てていて、確かに一番忙しいのは上司だな、着眼点が素晴らしいなと思いました。

奥井氏(JSOL)：（三井情報株式会社様へ）1点、逆に質問させてください。おそらく私たちは同じ課題を抱えていたわけで、外部検索のところでは中々精度がでないのでプロンプトを改善しなければならないこともあったと思うのですが、そのあたり開発の際はどのように工夫されていましたか？

外越氏（三井情報）： 本当に、プロンプトを試行錯誤するところはキリがないなと感じましたね。プロンプトを少し変えるだけで急に精度が上がったり下がったりしていたので、これはもっとプロンプトエンジニアリングを学ばないと欲しい情報を得ることができないなと感じていました。

奥井氏(JSOL)：そうですよね。逆にBTPによってAI構築の時間を短縮し、その分プロンプトエンジニアリングに時間を割くことができるのは開発基盤としてすごく魅力的だと私自身感じていました。

 

BTPの魅力はどのようなところにあるとお考えですか？

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）： まずはクラウドで動くので誰でもどこでもWeb上で開発ができるというところは便利だと感じています。また、BTPはSAP以外の環境とも連携できるのでそこも魅力的だと思います。

家原氏（三井情報）： いろいろなレベルの開発者が活用する事のできるものだと感じました。私自身、SAP ERP自体もBTPもあまり経験がない状態で今回のハッカソンに参加しましたが、実際に触ってみるとすごく直感的に動かすことができ、難しいコードを書かなくても実装できるなという点を感じたのでそこは魅力だと思います。

中尾氏（JSOL）：手軽かつ柔軟に拡張ができるところです。今回だとBuild Apps(※3)やBuild Process Automation(※4)を使いましたが、ローコード・ノーコードでの開発や標準で用意されたAPIを使用することで、他システムとデータ連携や拡張が容易にできたことに魅力を感じています。また、クラウドのプラットフォームとしてBTPは提供されているので、環境構築の必要がなく手軽にできるところも魅力だと思います。

 

今回、ファイナリストの多くのチームがBTPのAIを活用した作品を開発されていましたが、BTPのAIについてはどのような印象でしょうか？

外越氏（三井情報）：まず、手をつけ易いなと感じました。ラーニングコンテンツに従って操作するだけで簡単にAIが使える状態にまで構築できたので、「いかにAIを活用するか？」という点に集中できたのがよかったです。また、SAPの製品の中にある重要な業務データを活用でき、さらにその情報は学習には使われないと言われているところがとても精神的に頼りになるところだと感じています。

中村氏（JSOL）： 何よりSAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine(※5)が素晴らしいと思いました。Pythonを活用したベクトル化まで含めてこれまで全くAIを触ったことのない私でも少し習えば簡単にできてしまうところが素晴らしいテクノロジーだと感じました。

 

来年以降のハッカソンをもっと盛り上げていくために、何か運営に提言したいことがありましたら教えてください。

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：開発したソリューションを実際にライブで動かしているところをお見せできなかったのが残念でした。（今年度は運営の都合上、事前に撮影していただいたデモンストレーション動画で発表いただく形式をとっておりました）ライブで見ていただくことでより開発したソリューションの活用イメージなども湧いてきて、イベント自体も盛り上がってくると思いますので、来年に期待したいです。

矢作氏(三井情報）：ガチガチに緊張していた私が言うのもはばかれますが、もっともっと大きな会場でやりたいですね(笑)。大きい会場でお祭り感のあるイベントとして、もっとフランクな形で参加できるイベントになっていくとより大きなイベントになっていくと思います。

奥井氏(JSOL)：今日この場に来ても改めて思ったのですが、技術者同士でソリューションのことや開発中の困りごとをディスカッションができるのはとても面白いので、参加者間でよりディスカッションができる場があるといいのではないかと思いました。

 

最後に、BTPやハッカソンに新しく取り組んでみたいという方にメッセージをお願いいたします。

グエン・ロン・スエン氏（FPT）：開発の経験がない方でもぜひチャレンジしてみてほしいです。想像しているより難易度は低いと思いますので、実際にSAP Buildなどにたくさん触れてみて上達していくのがいいと思います。

家原氏（三井情報）： 意外とBTPに取り組むハードルは高くないぞ、ということを伝えたいです。お話を聞くと身構えてしまう方もいらっしゃると思うのですが、触ってみるとそうでもないなと感じることも多いと思います。なので、気軽にチャレンジして取り組んでいただきたいなと思います。

奥井氏(JSOL)：今回我々もBTP初心者が多かったのですが何も障害なく取り組めたのでぜひ挑戦していただきたいです。また、今回のBTPでの開発を通してSAPの他の製品やSAPの製品コンセプトといったことも学ぶことができたので、BTPをきっかけにこういったことを学ぶ機会にもしていただけたらと思います。

 

あとがき

今回のファイナリスト皆様へのインタビューを通じて、このイベントに対しての熱量と、BTP自体の盛り上がりを肌身で感じることができました。特に今回の座談会にご参加いただいた方は開発経験が豊富な方も多く、そのような方からBTPの魅力について直接お聞き出来たのはBTPの将来性を改めて感じることのできる瞬間でした。来年以降も、より多くのパートナー様、ユーザー様にご参加いただき、BTPを活用したイノベーションをともに創り上げていけることを願っております。（SAP 森本）

 

関連記事

SAP BTP Hackathon 2024 最優秀賞個別インタビュー 

SAP BTP Hackathon 2024 ファイナリストチーム座談会 Vol.1 

 

 

(※1)SAP Build：BTP上で生成 AI を活用してアプリケーション開発や自動化を加速するように設計された、ローコードおよびプロコードツールの製品群のこと。

https://www.sap.com/japan/products/technology-platform/build.html

(※2)Business Application Studio：：SAPソリューションを拡張するためのクラウドベースの開発ツール。プロコードでアプリケーションの開発ができる。

https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/sap-business-application-studio/develop?locale=ja-JP

(※3)Build Apps：ドラッグ＆ドロップを中心にマウス操作でアプリケーションを開発することができるローコード・ノーコードアプリケーション開発ツールのこと。

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/build-and-deploy-web-applications

(※4)Build Process Automation：ドラッグ & ドロップでワークフロープロセスとタスクを自動化することができるローコード・ノーコードアプリケーション開発ツールのこと。

https://www.sap.com/japan/products/technology-platform/process-automation.html

(※5)HANA Vector Engine：SAP HANA Cloud のマルチモデル エンジンに新たに追加された機能で、2つ以上のベクトル間の類似性を利用してビジネス上の問題を解決することができる。

https://news.sap.com/2024/04/sap-hana-cloud-vector-engine-ai-with-business-context/

entry.title: Transform Data with Javascript in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction :

  • Allows users to write executable code in JavaScript, leveraging pro-code capabilities for fusion development of enterprise automation scenarios.
  • Enables the execution of complex data type transformations and additional operations on the process context.
  • Previously it was limited to automation, now offers access to the entire context, including process inputs and custom variables, facilitating advanced data manipulation and transformation.
  • This activity comes with a new Script Editor which gives perfect IDE experience for user

Script Task Editor Features

  • Microsoft's Monaco based JS Code Editor
  • IntelliSense Support
  • Syntax highlighting
  • Type awareness
  • Console Panel- Collapsable & Expandable
  • Error Navigation
  • Other Editor Features:
    • Cut, copy, paste
    • Find all references
    • Go to definition
    • Code refactoring

Process Builder and Process Runtime  Restrictions

  • Allow till ES 5.1 standards
  • Restrict 'eval' and 'Function' constructor
  • Restrict direct manipulation of $.context, $.info, $.usertasks, allows only read
  • Read-only of input variables in context
  • Write possible only for custom variables in context
  • No addition/modification/deletion of input variables in $.context
  • Max 10,000 characters

Typical Use cases of Script Task:

  1. Array Iteration
  2. String manipulation
  3. Fetch Business Approval Status

Sample Scenario :

Lets take the below project scenario and see how Script task can come in handy.

  1. Fetch list of products from a backend system like S/4HANA 
  2. Iterate through the list of products and find the total price of all the items
  3. Concatenate Price and Currency of items which are otherwise coming from backend as two different attributes
  4. Send the total price and concatenated price and currency details as well to the approver for approval
  5. Fetch the business approval status (approved or rejected)
  6. Send Email to processor who has started the process with the approval details

The highlighted steps (ArrayList Iteration Script Task and Approval Status Script Task) are the steps pertaining to Script Task which will help with transforming the data , to find the total price of all items and concatenate price and currency (points 2 and 3 above).

Lets see in detail how the Script Tasks in this sample scenario has been created below :

  1. Custom variables are created to write the manipulated data and use it across the process 
  2. Process Input variables can be read but cannot be written to using Script task
  3. Process context up to previous steps can be read from the left side panel directly 
  4. When the context variables including Process inputs or Custom variables are chosen from the left side panel, automatically java script code for that gets populated in the right side of the editor where the code is actually written
  5. Script Editor comes with some sample code snippets to enable CzD also to start coding in java script.
  6. Intellisense support will help the user with recommendations as he types

Array Iteration Script Task:

  1. The items received from the previous Actions API call can be iterated through and the price of each item is summed up and total price is calculated 
  2. In the same iteration , the attribute values of price and currency are concatenated so that it can be sent across to the next step in the process which is the approval form

 

Fetch Approval Status Script Task:

  1. Business Approval status of whether the approval task has been approved or rejected can  be determined with this Script task
  2. $.usertasks ,$.info and $.roles allow access to identifiers of the current task or the exact execution.For more information on this, you can refer here
  3. We will make use of $.usertasks to fetch the business approval status
  4. $.usertasks has to be followed by the approval formId which can be obtained from intellisense or by clicking on one of the attributes of the approval form from left panel and replacing $.context with $.usertasks

 Isolated Code Execution - Testing

  • Easy to test written code even without process deployment.
  • Users can provide sample input values for the context variables used in the script.
  • Upon clicking the "Run Test" button, the code will be executed in the browser's JavaScript engine, and the result will be displayed instantly. 

 You can refer to the YouTube video on the same topic here for more details. 

Whats coming up - Further Enhancements :

  1. Generating the javascript code using Generative AI capability
  2. Reuse of Scripts

Happy Scripting and Transforming Data as per your need 😊

entry.title: "Dynamic" Email Body for Email Notification in SAP BTP Workflow or How to add a table dynamically
entry.content:
Intro

In this blog, we will present a simple, easy solution to address the issue of creating a dynamic email body and displaying multiple rows of a table in your static email notification.

We all know that we are moving to SAP Build Process Automation, but I want to address an issue that has happened to me multiple times in the Workflow in Business Application Studio. I recently discovered a workaround that can be useful to many developers.

We are talking about all the emails we send through the Workflow using the Mail Task. These emails usually refer to a static HTML file with elements from our context bound statically in the HTML. Example:

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
</head>
<body>
    <p><i>Simple example to use the content in the html.</i></p>

    <div class="table-container">
        <h4 class="section-title">TIME ENTRY REJECTION</h4>
        <p>You have <b>${context._week_info_to}</b> for Project <b>${context._project_id} – ${context._project_name}</b> that was rejected by <b>${context._wp_pm_name} (${context._wp_pm_id})</b> with the following comments (if any):</p>
        </div>
</body>
</html>

 

In our example, the use case is the following: a manager rejects a number of an employee's activities per week, and we want to send that groups all the rejected activities in a table within the email. We do not know how many activities there will be; it could be 1, 2, or 7. We want a nice and clean table that shows only the rejected rows, with no borders or fake information from others.

How can we achieve this?

Activities

  1. We need to discuss a limit with the stakeholders. In our case, it’s the maximum number of activities a manager can reject for that employee in a single week. We set the maximum at 10 (in your case, it could be 100 or 1,000)—it's the maximum number of rows in your table.

  2. Modify the HTML code to include all the lines up to the limit we set before, paying special attention to the email's CSS.

  3. Develop a good JS script task to prepare our email context and set the correct CSS attributes.

Limit of elements, the starting point

To create truly dynamic content in an email, we would typically use JavaScript. However, this is generally considered a bad idea due to security concerns, and it's not a feature that's commonly available, especially given SAP's strong focus on security. Instead, we use a semi-dynamic approach.

This involves adding as many rows as possible to the table, safe in the knowledge that HTML doesn't produce errors if something is undefined. In these cases, we will simply have an empty space.

To implement this, we must clarify the table's limits with the stakeholders. If necessary, we can include a 100% buffer.

In our example scenario, the limit is 10 rejected requests, so I will set it to 20.

The HTML

As I said before the html will include all the possible lines + buffer. And we will also consider in our lines the css value that permit to hide and show the row that we are not going to show.

 

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Time Rejection Notification</title>
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            margin: 0;
            padding: 20px;
            background-color: #f4f4f9;
        }
        h4, h3 {
            color: #333;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        p {
            color: #555;
            line-height: 1.6;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        b {
            color: #333;
        }
        .section-title {
            border-bottom: 2px solid #333;
            padding-bottom: 5px;
            margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        .table-container {
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            padding: 10px;
            background-color: #fff;
            border: 1px solid #ddd;
            border-radius: 5px;
        }
        table {
            width: 100%;
            border-collapse: collapse;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
        th, td {
            border: 1px solid #dddddd;
            text-align: left;
            padding: 8px;
        }
        th {
            background-color: #f2f2f2;
        }
        .hidden-row {
            display: none;
        }
        .footer {
            font-size: 0.8em;
            color: #888;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <p><i>Please review the rejected time entries below and resubmit as necessary.</i></p>

    <div class="table-container">
        <h4 class="section-title">TIME ENTRY REJECTION</h4>
        <p>You have time for the period ending <b>${context._week_info_to}</b> for Project <b>${context._project_id} – ${context._project_name}</b> that was rejected by <b>${context._wp_pm_name} (${context._wp_pm_id})</b> with the following comments (if any):</p>

        <table border="1">
            <thead>
                <tr>
                    <th>Week Ending</th>
                    <th>Project ID</th>
                    <th>Project Description</th>
                    <th>WP Name</th>
                    <th>Prop Hours</th>
                    <th>Activity Type Desc</th>
                    <th>Note</th>
                </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[0].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[0]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[1].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[1]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[2].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[2]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[3].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[3]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[4].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[4]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[5].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[5]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[6].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[6]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[7].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[7]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[8].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[8]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[9].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[9]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[10].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[10]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[11].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[11]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[12].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[12]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[13].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[13]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[14].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[14]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[15].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[15]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[16].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[16]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[17].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[17]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[18].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[18]._note}</td>
                </tr>
                <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[19].class}>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19].z_date}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._project_id}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._project_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._wp_name}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._prop_hours}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._activity_type_desc}</td>
                    <td>${context.rejectedEntries[19]._note}</td>
                </tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>  

        <p>“Please submit time only for the time that has been rejected from the app <a href=${context.link} target="_blank">My Inbox</a>. Reach out to your Work Package manager with any questions. Thank you.”</p>
    </div>

    
</body>
</html>

 

You see that each line of the table <tr class=${context.rejectedEntries[count].class} > has ready the attribute class, and it's dynamically bonded with the maximum number of rows we decided at the beginning of the previous step. You can also notice that I added as a CSS class definition the following CSS code: 

.hidden-row {
            display: none;
        }

This code will simple help to hide the row we are not using, we will use this class in the next session. 

JS Script

Now, we need to create our JS code. We set this code usually in the script task right before sending the email:
Script Task

var entries = $.context._time_entries; //Array of what we want to send in the email

var rejectedEntries = [];
for (count = 0; count < 20; count++) {
	if(count >= entries.length){
		rejectedEntries.push({ class : "hidden-row" });
	} else {
		entries[count].class = "";
		rejectedEntries.push(entries[count]);
	}
}

$.context._time_entries = rejectedEntries;

This is a simple loop, but it contains a hidden logic.
During each iteration, we increment the index variable count regardless of whether our array of entries has ended or not. We continue this loop until we reach the maximum number of rows we specified at the beginning in this blog. For example, if we have 4 valid entries to write in our table ( entries.length = 4 ), for all those four entries, we set the class property equal to empty (line 8). When our count points to an undefined element (ideally when our index is greater than the length of the array), we set the class property for that row to "hidden-row".
After this procedure, we can imagine our JSON array to be similar to this:

[
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-01",
        "_project_id": "101",
        "_project_name": "Project Sunrise",
        "_wp_name": "Initial Planning",
        "_prop_hours": "4",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Planning",
        "_note": "Kick-off meeting with the client"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-02",
        "_project_id": "102",
        "_project_name": "Project Apollo",
        "_wp_name": "Requirements Gathering",
        "_prop_hours": "6",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Analysis",
        "_note": "Requirement documentation"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-03",
        "_project_id": "103",
        "_project_name": "Project Legacy",
        "_wp_name": "Design",
        "_prop_hours": "3",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Design",
        "_note": "Initial design sketches"
    },
    {
        "class": "",
        "z_date": "2024-10-04",
        "_project_id": "104",
        "_project_name": "Project Titan",
        "_wp_name": "Development",
        "_prop_hours": "5",
        "_activity_type_desc": "Coding",
        "_note": "Development of core modules"
    },
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"},
    {"class": "hidden-row"}
]

Now you can connect all the dots: if you send the email with this structure, the rows after the first 4 will be hidden automatically without creating an unsightly and unnecessary empty row that could disrupt the visual appearance of your email.

Conclusion

By integrating HTML and JavaScript in SAP Build Process Automation ( Workflow in BAS), you can effectively create dynamic, visually appealing email notifications that adapt to varying amounts of data. Whether you're notifying employees of time entry rejections or any other updates, this approach provides a "flexible" solution for your HTML email. Even if in the future we will use low-code/no-code

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing and role of Artificial Intelligence
entry.content:
The manufacturing industry has undergone a significant transformation, progressing from mechanical to electrical to more abstract concepts. With advancements in technologies such as artificial intelligence, we are witnessing the emergence of intelligent, self-operating systems. While sophisticated technologies like robotics, machine learning, and the Internet of Things are not entirely new in manufacturing, their integration into production lines, both in discrete and process manufacturing, has been steadily growing.

Thanks to developments in Generative AI, organizations are vying to become intelligent enterprises at a breakneck pace. Petabytes of data and millions of smart sensors have made it challenging for big OEMs to use the data meaningfully so that they can act on the data when necessary and gain insights.

SAP Digital Manufacturing solution offers a range of impressive features and functionalities for manufacturing execution and analytics. It facilitates smart factory use cases where intelligent technologies can drive organizations toward greater autonomy, resilience, and sustainability.

SAP's strategic integration of embedded AI into business processes is revolutionizing operations for many organizations that have been deterred by the complexities of developing, maintaining, and deploying AI applications.

One particularly promising application of AI in manufacturing is visual inspection for quality control. Numerous organizations across various industries, including automotive, consumer products, food and beverage, and high-tech manufacturing, are already harnessing the power of machine learning for this purpose. However, generative AI and computer vision hold even greater potential for identifying defects and logging non-conformances in non-traditional and highly customized manufacturing processes.

Another area where many organizations face challenges is manufacturing analytics. The demand for real-time access to relevant manufacturing data on a unified platform is driving the need for innovative solutions. SAP Digital Manufacturing offers a comprehensive solution for deploying AI and ML models on both structured and unstructured data. This foundation enables essential analytics for operational insights and live monitoring, which are crucial for every manufacturing organization.

This architecture empowers users to visualize critical trends and metrics in a single dashboard, eliminating the need to log into multiple systems. The no-code application build platform within SAP Digital Manufacturing allows users to create custom dashboards and leverage SAP Analytics Cloud for embedded analytics. Additionally, features like query designer facilitate the easy creation of key performance indicators (KPIs) based on specific logic.

Line reporting, on the other hand, provides real-time visibility into shop floor processes through Production Operator Dashboards (PODs). Line Monitor PODs enable shop floor schedulers to visualize the execution of shop floor control orders (SFCs) in real time and respond to alerts, scrap, or quality-related non-conformances.

Manufacturing automation is becoming increasingly sophisticated due to technological advancements. This automation has revolutionized the production of goods, boosting efficiency, quality, and innovation. By minimizing human error, automation contributes to higher-quality products and lower defect rates. SAP Digital Manufacturing provides a powerful automation platform, including manufacturing APIs and services, to automate manufacturing processes and integrate different plant systems seamlessly, fostering a connected manufacturing environment.

SAP Digital Manufacturing offers flexible extension options, including "In-App" and "Side-by-side" extension features, enabling seamless integration with other systems and data harmonization. The solution also supports the integration of external machine learning models developed and trained outside of Digital Manufacturing.

Custom-built business services or APIs can be easily integrated into the standard production process. Process extensions may require custom integration flows within the SAP BTP-based integration suite, incorporating various BAPIs, OData, and REST services to connect with digital manufacturing.

The potential of AI in manufacturing and supply chains is immense. Use cases include error identification and root cause analysis, simulation, and outlier detection, and the transformative "actions-to-insights" approach. By integrating AI with process automation and other technologies, organizations can effectively leverage "insights-to-action" capabilities.

To learn more about how SAP Digital Manufacturing will revolutionize your operations, order your copy today. 

Authors : Rahul Kumar (rahul.kumar04@sap.com) , Rohit Dwivedi (r.dwivedi@sap.com) 

https://www.sap-press.com/introducing-sap-digital-manufacturing_5948/

entry.title: Enhance Task Approvals with the Power of AI
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced corporate environment, delays in task approvals can significantly hamper productivity, create bottlenecks, and escalate costs. When approval processes are slow, employees often face frustration, morale dips, and businesses run the risk of missing critical opportunities or failing to meet regulatory compliance deadlines. Particularly in industries that thrive on innovation, these delays can obstruct competitiveness and diminish the organization’s ability to respond swiftly to market changes.

To address these challenges, Task Center is launching a revolutionary AI-powered feature called Intelligent Recommendations. This functionality leverages artificial intelligence to streamline and accelerate task approval processes by analyzing key attributes relevant to specific business cases and generating predictive models for task approvals.

The initial version of Intelligent Recommendations focuses on tasks created using SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)—a cloud-based workflow management solution—with plans to expand compatibility with other providers in future updates.

Prerequisites:

To use the Intelligent Recommendations feature, the SBPA service must be instantiated within the tenant, as the AI engine resides there. This initial implementation only supports process automation through SBPA, utilizing data that can be accessed from the SBPA engine.

User Perspectives on Intelligent Recommendations

Business User:

From the business user’s perspective, Intelligent Recommendations operates on a per-task-type level. A critical element of this functionality is the Confidence Level, which determines the AI’s confidence in automatically approving tasks based on the specified criteria.

Task Type Selection:
Begin by selecting the desired task type and clicking the "Go" button. This action will display the confidence level field, where approval conditions can be defined.    

Setting Confidence Levels:
Users can specify a confidence level for automatic task approvals. If the confidence level falls below 70%, the task will not qualify for automatic approval. Available options for defining the confidence level include: Less than,Equal to, Between and Greater than. 

                     

Note:
Clicking the "Go" button is essential when setting up the confidence level, as this ensures the changes are applied correctly and helps optimize system performance. 

 

Sorting by Confidence Levels:

Once the confidence level is set, it becomes a sortable column within the task table. This enables users to prioritize tasks based on their confidence level, facilitating a more organized and efficient approval process

Administrator View: Managing Intelligent Recommendations

Administration Cockpit:

Administrators have access to the Intelligent Recommendations tab within the Task Center’s administration cockpit. This section provides a comprehensive overview of all models, allowing administrators to create, manage, and adjust AI-powered task approval models as business requirements evolve.

Steps to Create a Model:

  1. Accessing the Model Creation Feature:
    To create a new model, Go to Administration cockpit  and Select Intelligent Reccomendations tab. click on the "Create Model" button.                                   

    Please note that only authorized administrators or key users can perform this action, so ensure the appropriate user roles are in place.

  2. Filling in the Model Details:
    Upon clicking "Create Model," a pop-up window will appear with the following fields: 
      • Task Provider: At present, only SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) is available as a task provider.
      • Task Type: Select the appropriate task type from the available dropdownlist.
      • Model Name: Provide a unique name for the model, which will be used to identify it in the list of models.
      • Description: Enter a description that reflects the model's purpose from administrative point of view.                                                      
  3. Selecting Task Type Attributes:
    Administrators can choose from a list of available task attributes:
    • Categorical Attributes: These are static attributes that remain consistent ver time.
    • Numerical Attributes: Dynamic attributes that may change, reflecting variations in the business environment.
  4. Completing the Model Creation:
    After configuring the model, click "Create". The newly created model will appear in the list with its initial status as either Active or In Training.                    
  5. Status and Activation:
    Once the AI engine has analyzed all relevant tasks, the model’s status will update to Ready for Activation. A Benchmark value will be displayed, indicating the model’s reliability and effectiveness. This benchmark is calculated based on several factors, and the data science team can provide more details on its derivation.
  6. Model Management:
    • Activation: Once ready, the model can be activated and put into use. If needed, models can also be deactivated to accommodate shifting business needs.
    • Retraining the Model: If the benchmark is unsatisfactory, administrators can retain the model by adjusting the selected attributes. This flexibility ensures that the model remains relevant as business requirements evolve.
  7. Conclusion:

    The Intelligent Recommendations feature within Task Center introduces an AI-driven approach to task approvals, providing businesses with the ability to significantly reduce bottlenecks, enhance operational efficiency, and maintain compliance with critical deadlines. By allowing both business users and administrators to define confidence levels, manage models, and retrain them as necessary, this functionality empowers organizations to stay agile and competitive in an increasingly fast-paced market.

    As more providers are integrated into future releases, the scope of Intelligent Recommendations will continue to grow, offering even more opportunities for businesses to harness the power of AI in their workflows.

     

    Product Links:

    https://help.sap.com/docs/task-center/sap-task-center/working-with-intelligent-recommendations

    https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/evergreen-assets/safelinks/1/atp-safelinks.html

    Enjoy!!

entry.title: Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • Motivation for Guided Experiences

  • New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

  • Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

  • Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

  • What's Next?

  • Additional Read

Motivation for Guided Experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved, enabling them to resume from where they left off. Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment. To learn more about the background & motivation around guided experiences, please see our previous blog about this capability.

New Guided Process Modelling: Advanced Automation and Flexibility

The major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. The following key aspects have been added:

Guided Process Artefact

The new ‘Guided Process’ artefact allows you to design and manage structured, multi-step processes within SAP Build Process Automation. This feature is perfect for creating more dynamic and reusable workflows.

Stages and Steps 

Following the structure introduced for the wizard layout, ‘Guided Processes’ equally allow to create stages & steps within each stage.  Additionally, these process steps can be designed to be sequential or parallel flows, allowing for both straightforward and complex workflows.

Create a StageAdd a Step to a Stage

 

Note: with this release, only SAP Build Work Zone is supported as ‘Step UI’; meaning the step itself needs to designed via the Work Zone wizard layout editor (for example to add UI integration cards).

Advanced Logic Integration

To create more sophisticated processes as basis for your guided experience, branches can be used to structure the overall guided process and duration checks can be incorporated via wait for duration to handle conditional starting of stages. Furthermore, subprocesses can be used to handle more complex requirements, such as retrieving hire date information.

Easy Deployment to SAP Build Work Zone

Once the guided process is modelled, it can be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone as a wizard layout template, ensuring easy availability for end-users. This provides a seamless transition from process design to execution.

Note: the deployment to SAP Build Work Zone is performed on behalf of the logged-in user via the automatically created destination pointing to DWS as per the following guide.

Shared Environments in SAP Build

This new ‘Guided Process’ artefact is only supported in shared environments, not in the default / public one. To learn more about shared environments in SAP Build, please refer to the following blog post.

Connecting Guided Processes as Wizard Templates in SAP Build Work Zone

In addition to modelling ‘wizard layouts’ directly in SAP Build Work Zone (which is now available via the “New Wizard” option)...

Add Wizard LayoutSelect Process for WizardOption for Standalone Wizard

 

… it is now possible to select ‘Guided Processes’ modelled in SAP Build Process Automation as the underlying structure when creating guided experiences.

Guided Process-based Wizards

Users can now select one of previously deployed guided processes they have access to. These serve as the template the wizard layout within SAP Build Work Zone.

Note: While modelling & deploying guided processes via the SAP Build Lobby requires access to the ‘advanced user’ license in SAP Build Process Automation, that is not the case for consuming deployed processes. For doing so, the user requires the following permissions:

  • Workpage edit permissions in Work Zone to edit the workpage, add wizard layouts,
  • ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection
  • 'Execute' permission for shared environment (Share an Environment | SAP Help Portal)

Note: creating wizard layouts based on ‘guided processes’ is currently released in preview environments of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and will become available in the non-preview environments with the subsequent release.

Auto-generated Wizard Structure

When selecting a guided process, the wizard layout is automatically generated based on the process definition, saving time and ensuring consistency.

Design Wizard with Widgets & UI Cards

Just like with a manually-created one, you can customize guided process-based wizard layouts using different widgets and UI cards, providing flexibility in designing the user experience.

  • Adding Widgets / Cards to each step: 
  • Example Step with Image widget:

Note: Already in a previous release, UI cards have been enhanced to interact with wizard layouts in SAP Build Work Zone, for instance to enforce users taking certain actions. Details and code examples for such UI cards are available via our central SAP Build Work Zone GitHub sample repository.

Process Instance Creation

When a user starts a wizard based on a guided process, an instance of that process is started automatically, allowing execution & resuming of the steps according to the defined process structure.

Conclusion – SAP Build Guided Experiences

This streamlined integration reduces development time and ensures that teams can leverage the power of SAP Build Process Automation alongside SAP Build Work Zone bringing exciting possibilities for creating powerful & content-rich guided experiences. These innovations not only simplify process design and execution but also ensure that you can deploy efficient, scalable guided experiences tailored to your unique needs – bringing different capabilities from SAP Build together via a deeply integrated, end-to-end experience!

What’s Next?

Looking ahead, we are planning to introduce additional features to further enhance guided experiences. These upcoming capabilities aim to add additional flexibility for creating, designing & operating guided experiences with SAP Build.  Here's a sneak peek at what's planned (*):

Spoiler
  • Repeating Guided Experiences
  • Starting Multiple Instances of the same Guided Experience
  • Support for more Step UIs, including Forms
  • Integration with Process Visibility for Monitoring
  • […]

In addition to those additional enhancements, we are also working on providing additional pre-built business content (packages) for guided experiences (*), such as onboarding in context of SAP SuccessFactors. Stay tuned as these new features roll out, offering even more ways to optimize your guided experiences and streamline your business processes!

(*) Disclaimer: Please note that the roadmap items shared here are subject to change and may not be delivered as initially planned. The information provided is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a commitment by SAP. As always, the best resource to learn about upcoming capabilities is the central roadmap explorer.

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone Preview: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/what... (will move to regular admin guide with next release)
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard

entry.title: Your guide to Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build - Frequently asked questions
entry.content:
Welcome everyone! 

In this blogpost, I will breakdown the most frequently asked questions about guided experiences, an innovative capability powered by SAP Build. I will address questions around how it works, the benefits it offers, common use cases and much more. Let’s get started on unlocking the full potential of guided experiences. 

🔐For those looking to dive deeper into the capability, I recommend exploring our expert blogpost, where you'll find a comprehensive breakdown of its features. 

***

✔️ What are guided experiences in SAP Build?

Guided experience is a capability within SAP Build, a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This capability enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. It allows users to create interactive, step-by-step guides for navigating and using applications.

✔️ What are the key aspects of guided experiences?

Key aspects of guided experiences are as below. 

  • Step-by-step guidance: Users are guided through a series of steps to complete a specific task or process performed within SAP Build Work Zone with use cases often spanning across multiple systems. 
  • Customizable content: Users can create custom content for the guided experiences, including text, images, and videos, to provide clear and concise instructions, with no- and low-code capabilities.
  • Interactive elements: Guided experiences can include elements like UI Cards and default widgets (image, text, content, etc.) to engage users and ensure smooth execution.

✔️ What are some common use cases for guided experiences?

Some use cases examples can be: 

  • Creating one simple employee onboarding journey for new employees
  • Creating step-based guide to deliver best practices, training and relevant material to empower sales teams
  • Simplified purchase order verification process while purchasing equipment
  • Creating a smooth onboarding journey for new suppliers
  • Seamless journey for processing invoices across channel partners
  • And many more...

✔️Which SAP Build solutions are included in guided experiences?

Guided experiences incorporate capabilities from all the low-code SAP Build solutions - SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

✔️ How does guided experiences drive business value?

  • Enhanced User Experience: Empowers workforces with personalized digital experiences, boosting satisfaction, engagement, and retention.
  • Increased Productivity: Automates tasks and streamlines processes, enabling employees to focus on high-impact work.
  • Greater Agility: Rapidly deploys, modifies, and scale digital capabilities with intuitive tools, empowering business builders without IT dependency

✔️What is a 'wizard layout' in guided experiences?

Wizard layout the actual user interface in SAP Build Work Zone that the end-user work with. It allows users to create a step-by-step experience on any workpage. This layout can consist of one or multiple stages, each containing various steps. These steps or stages can be set as optional or mandatory, depending on the process requirements. Users can incorporate different UI elements available in Work Zone, such as tiles, widgets, UI cards, and other applications into each step.

✔️ What is a 'guided process' in guided experiences?

For more advanced processes, it is possible to create a ‘guided process’ as an artefact in SAP Build Process Automation and make use of the powerful capabilities of Process Automation. The guided process can then be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone and then used as the foundation/template when creating a wizard layout as an alternative option to creating the wizard manually directly in Work Zone.

✔️ What authorization is required for a user to create a Guided Experience in SAP Build Work Zone using a predefined template?

The user must have end-user permission/authorization. Any user with the permission to create and edit a work page can modify and create wizards within SAP Build Work Zone. When creating a guided experience wizard layout based on a ‘guided process’ template, the user requires access to a deployed template in either the public or another environment in Process Automation. To model and deploy the ‘guided process’ artefact, the advanced user permission is required in Process Automation side.

✔️ Can a user save their progress in SAP Build Work Zone for gradual competition?

Yes, progress of the user within the wizard layout is saved automatically so that they return and complete the steps another time – if needed.

✔️ Does the user mark completion of their own tasks or does Work Zone pick up on the status from other systems?

Work Zone has the ability to verify if a specific activity has been completed by the user. For mandatory steps, Work Zone ensures that the task is completed before allowing progress in the process. In each step, tasks / activities from other business systems could be incorporated via UI5 cards (where the task would then be completed). The UI card framework has also been enhanced to support interaction with the wizard layout to check the completion of a task. See here!

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition?

No, they are not available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and there are currently no plans to enable this capability in standard edition either.

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

 Yes, additionally to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, they are also available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. 

✔️ Given the visibility scenarios in SAP Build Process Automation, what additional benefits does SAP Build Work Zone offer?

SAP Build Work Zone provides the consumption layer and easy wizard editor that can be conveniently redefined by content administrator at runtime. With SAP Build Process Automation ‘guided process’ as the process backbone for the wizard layout, the flexible design & content capabilities of Work Zone provide the user-facing capabilities. Additionally, aspects like UI5 cards enable flexible, context-rich integration of business data across SAP and 3rd party solutions in each individual wizard step.

✔️ Is it possible to for the end-user to restart the completed process/step?

Capabilities to restart a completed process are on the roadmap, which can then be enabled in the guided experience configuration. Currently a guided experience can enforce a restart when editing and publishing a new version; alternatively, it can be configured to allow existing processes to complete with the previous version until completion.

✔️ What guided experience capabilities are on the roadmap?

Looking ahead into the H2 2024 and beyond, many additional capabilities are planned for the SAP Build guided experience capability. For instance integration with process visibility, support for additional step UIs like forms, ability for users to start multiple guided experiences for the same template and more.

Further to this, additional business content examples – for example onboarding from SAP SuccessFactors – are being created to get started more easily. This business content can then be used as template and tweaked as required.

***

I hope this blogpost has addressed your questions. If you want to learn more, feel free to explore the additional resources listed below.

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Build Process Automation on: sap.com / SAP Community 

➤ SAP Builders Community Group 

➤ Learning Journey: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning

➤ Learning Journey: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

➤ SAP TechEd session ft. guided experiences: (AD108) Enhance Business Efficiency with SAP Build 

➤ SAP Devtoberfest session: Guided Experiences with SAP Build

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Environments October Update
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Few months ago we introduced the Environments in this article. We've just released a significant update with the support of the following artifacts:

  • Process
  • Decision
  • Forms
  • Visibility Scenario

What it means for you?

You can now deploy a project containing a process within a Shared Environment. Only users who have access to a specific environment can use it. You can leverage the "Execute" privilege for enhancing the security and visibility of deployed Forms and Processes.

Two tabs have been added within the Control Tower > Environments for accessing Visibility Scenarios and Processes:

As explained before, now when deploying a new version, only the latest version will be seen as "active" within a Shared Environment. The behavior remains the same within the Public Environment.

For more details, our SAP Note has been updated with some useful information: https://me.sap.com/notes/3394899

entry.title: The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?
entry.content:
When creating clean core extensions for SAP S/4HANA, you are confronted with a vast number of powerful tools. The tools can be divided into four quarters by the dimensions on-stack versus side-by-side and low code versus pro-code, which is shown in the following figure:

The set of available tools may be overwhelming at first sight, so how to find the right one? And – if you found the right tool, how do you find the right API or extension point to extend the application you are interested in?

Of course, there are documents that provide guidance on when to use what (see for example in the document Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions , Chapter 3, “When To Use What”), but it is definitely better to get the guidance right in the system when creation the extension. SAP is investing a lot in implementing guidance in the system. The investment goes along two paths:

  • Investment in AI support (Joule)
  • Investment in wizards

Many new wizards are available in the ABAP platform, for example for creating a new RAP BO or for creating custom fields. For a full picture, I refer to other documents and presentations given during TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

In this blog post I want to introduce the Extensibility Wizard. This wizard guides you from the application through the creation of the respective extension. Here is an example (instead of reading the following lines, you can also watch my demo in my TechEd or Devtoberfest session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility 😞

  1. Assume that you are in your SAP S/4HANA application. For my example I choose the application Manage Journal Entries.
  2. Open the Fiori Launchpad context menu (click your avatar). If the Extensibility Wizard is configures (see link to the documentation with the setup guide at the end of this post), you can see the menu entry Create Extension, and you can click it (Figure 2).

                  
                  Figure 2

  1. The Extensibility Wizard opens and shows the different extensions tasks, that you can create for this application. As of 2408, the following extensions tasks are included:
    1. Custom Fields
    2. Custom Logic
    3. Custom Process
    4. The figure below shows – as a labs preview – also the extensibility task Custom Application, which is not yet available in your 2408 release.

               
               Figure 3

  1. Now let us select the extensibility task Custom Process. The next steps of the wizard now guide us through the creation of a new process in SAP Build Process Integration. As the wizard was started from the Manage Journal Entries application, it knows which triggers are relevant for this application, and offers for example the event JournalEntry Created as the trigger for a new process. As a final step, the wizard creates the new process in SAP Build and let you navigate to SAP Build Lobby and directly into the process. (Please see the complete flow in my session What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility )

I hope that this example gives you an idea on how the Extensibility Wizard works.

As mentioned, in 2408, the Extensibility Wizard includes – apart from the Custom Process – two more tasks:

  • The Custom Field task opens the Custom Fields Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create a custom field. Also here, the business context is prefilled according to the application from where you start the wizard.
  • The Custom Logic task opens the Custom Logic Fiori app in your SAP S/4HANA tenant, where you can create custom logic. Also here, the business context is prefilled.

If you are used to open the Custom Fields and the Custom Logic apps via the UI Adaption Mode (menu entry Adapt UI, as you can see in Figure 2), you can of course still do this.

In future additional extensibility items and features will be added, for example:

  • For the extensibility item Custom Process, additional trigger options will be provided.
  • A new extensibility item Custom Application will be added, which guides you through the creation of a new application with SAP Build Code.

Curious to see the wizard in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant? Configure it today, see the documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.
